---
title: "US State Clustering Analysis"
author: "Roger Blake"
format: revealjs
editor: visual
---

## Setup {visibility="hidden"}

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Load libraries
library(factoextra)
library(knitr)

# 1. Prepare Base Data
df <- as.data.frame(state.x77)
vars_to_keep <- c("Income", "Illiteracy", "Life Exp", "Murder", "HS Grad", "Frost", "Area")
df_subset <- df[, vars_to_keep]

# Scale data
df_scaled <- scale(df_subset)

# 2. Add Built-in Census Data for comparison later
df_subset$Census_Region <- state.region      
df_subset$Census_Division <- state.division
```

## Following Census Regions {.scrollable}
:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="65%"}
```{r}
# Run k-means for k=4
set.seed(123)
km_4 <- kmeans(df_scaled, centers = 4, nstart = 25)
df_subset$Cluster_k4 <- as.factor(km_4$cluster)

# Visualize k=4
fviz_cluster(km_4, data = df_scaled, main = "K-Means (k=4)")
```
:::

::: {.column width="35%" style="font-size: 0.55em;"}
```{r}
# Create a mapping dataframe
state_map_4 <- data.frame(State = rownames(df_subset), Cluster = df_subset$Cluster_k4)

# Aggregate states into a comma-separated list by cluster
table_4 <- aggregate(State ~ Cluster, data = state_map_4, FUN = function(x) paste(x, collapse = ", "))

# Print clean table
kable(table_4, col.names = c("Cluster", "States Included"))
```
:::
::::
## Elbow method {.scrollable}
:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="65%"}
```{r}
# Subset data to exclude Alaska and Hawaii
exclude_states <- c("Alaska", "Hawaii")
df_lower48 <- df_subset[!(rownames(df_subset) %in% exclude_states), ]
df_scaled_lower48 <- scale(df_lower48[, vars_to_keep])

# Run K=5 with Lower 48 states
set.seed(123)
km_5_lower48 <- kmeans(df_scaled_lower48, centers = 5, nstart = 25)

# Visualize Lower 48 Clusters
fviz_cluster(km_5_lower48, data = df_scaled_lower48, main = "Optimal (k=5) - Lower 48")
```
:::

::: {.column width="35%" style="font-size: 0.55em;"}
```{r}
# Create mapping for lower 48
state_map_5 <- data.frame(State = rownames(df_lower48), Cluster = as.factor(km_5_lower48$cluster))

# Aggregate and print
table_5 <- aggregate(State ~ Cluster, data = state_map_5, FUN = function(x) paste(x, collapse = ", "))
kable(table_5, col.names = c("Cluster", "States Included"))
```
:::

::::
## Adding in weighted location {.scrollable}
:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="65%"}
```{r}
# Create spatial dataset
df_geo <- df_subset[, vars_to_keep]
df_geo$Longitude <- state.center$x
df_geo$Latitude <- state.center$y
df_geo_scaled <- scale(df_geo)

# Run k=4 with spatial awareness
set.seed(123)
km_geo_4 <- kmeans(df_geo_scaled, centers = 4, nstart = 25)
df_subset$Geo_Cluster_4 <- as.factor(km_geo_4$cluster)

# Visualize Spatially-Aware k=4
fviz_cluster(km_geo_4, data = df_geo_scaled, main = "Geo-Weighted K-Means")
```
:::

::: {.column width="35%" style="font-size: 0.55em;"}
```{r}
# Create mapping for geo clusters
state_map_geo <- data.frame(State = rownames(df_subset), Cluster = df_subset$Geo_Cluster_4)

# Aggregate and print
table_geo <- aggregate(State ~ Cluster, data = state_map_geo, FUN = function(x) paste(x, collapse = ", "))
kable(table_geo, col.names = c("Cluster", "States Included"))
```
:::
::::
